name: Testing with every write
purpose: Mandates multi-level test writing (unit, integration, deletion, regression) for every code change — running existing tests is not sufficient.
notes: originSessionId: 142f84e3-1dc8-4c4e-87ec-a1102adba1df. References Session #286 and SoNash-specific script names (runReconcileCommits, generateReviewId, check-review-record.js, rotate-jsonl.js) as the incident context. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home (feedback_testing_with_writes.md) with near-identical content. No canonical counterpart in SoNash. The 4-level testing taxonomy (unit/integration/deletion/regression) is fully portable.
portable_elements: Full 4-level testing taxonomy is portable. Smoke-testing-is-not-testing principle is universal. Deletion-test pattern (verify nothing breaks when removing functionality) is often overlooked and particularly high-value.

name: Never skip user-action steps in plans
purpose: When a plan step requires user action (install, auth, credentials), stop and prompt the user — do not treat it as documentation-only and move on.
notes: No originSessionId in frontmatter. References /deep-research P1 Step 13 (npm install -g @google/gemini-cli + Google auth) as the incident — Gemini CLI was never actually installed. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with near-identical content. No canonical counterpart in SoNash. The rule (user-action steps are blocking; documented requirement != met requirement) is fully portable.
portable_elements: Entire rule portable. 'Documented requirement does not equal met requirement' is universally applicable. The blocking pattern (step is NOT done until user confirms) is particularly important for install/auth steps in any plan execution.

name: Preferred workflow chain
purpose: Defines user's fixed workflow chain (brainstorm → deep-research → deep-plan → execute via GSD) and prohibits substituting 'superpowers' skills.
sanitize_fields: superpowers skill references (SoNash-specific: writing-plans, subagent-driven-development, executing-plans)
GSD reference (JASON-OS uses different execution path)
notes: No originSessionId. References 'superpowers:writing-plans', 'superpowers:subagent-driven-development' skills — SoNash-specific plugin ecosystem. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with near-identical content. No canonical counterpart. Content-bleed risk: the specific superpowers skill names and path conflict (docs/superpowers/plans/ vs .claude/plans/) are SoNash-specific. The workflow chain pattern itself is portable but execution endpoint differs between projects (JASON-OS does not use GSD).
portable_elements: Workflow chain taxonomy (brainstorm → deep-research → deep-plan → execute) is portable. The 'do not intercept the chain' principle is portable with updated skill names. The specific conflict description (superpowers skills) is SoNash context.

name: Worktree workflow guidance
purpose: Defines when to use worktrees (research/planning, read-heavy) vs branches (implementation, commits, PRs), documents limitations, and identifies .worktreeinclude as the sync mechanism.
notes: No originSessionId. Documents that .claude/skills/, .claude/agents/, .claude/rules/ are NOT copied to worktrees. References 'custom worktree skill created (deferred task)' — SoNash-specific deferred item. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with near-identical content. No canonical counterpart. Key technical constraints (hooks/skills/state not in worktrees, 1M context CWD drift) are Claude Code platform facts — fully portable.
portable_elements: Full guidance portable. EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree as built-in tools (not skills/agents), .worktreeinclude mechanism, default-to-branches heuristic — all universal Claude Code patterns.

name: Write rejection = hard stop
purpose: When Write/Edit is rejected by a hook, immediately retry via Bash/Python heredoc — never proceed to next phase with missing artifacts.
notes: originSessionId: 331dfa27-c52d-46cc-91ce-e5b7c20e8329. References Session #276 and security_reminder_hook as incident context — SoNash-specific hook name. The 3-step recovery pattern (check result → Bash/python3 heredoc → verify non-empty) is fully portable. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with near-identical content. No canonical counterpart. The insight that analysis artifacts discussing security topics trigger false positives is broadly applicable.
portable_elements: 3-step recovery pattern fully portable. Python3 heredoc as hook bypass technique is universal. Don't-proceed-on-missing-artifact principle applies to any project.

name: Custom agents — COMPLETE
purpose: Tracks completion of the custom-agents initiative (Session #255, PR #487): 38 agents, 6 pipeline agents, sonash-context skill, corrections on effort:max and auto-delegation.

Key corrections still relevant: effort:max → use opus for heavy agents; agent names case-insensitivity UNCONFIRMED; auto-delegation is pure LLM reasoning (design for explicit). These corrections are portable.
portable_elements: Three key technical corrections are portable: effort:max causes API error (use model: opus), agent name case-insensitivity unconfirmed (design lowercase), auto-delegation is not reliable (design for explicit invocation). 6 pipeline agent types (verifier, contrarian-challenger, otb-challenger, dispute-resolver, gap-pursuer, final-synthesizer) are the JASON-OS deep-research agent set.

name: Hook if-conditions research and implementation
purpose: Research and 3-wave implementation of hook if-conditions: 21 decisions, all waves complete. Documents key technical facts about Claude Code hook behavior.
sanitize_fields: wave-specific hook names (lean wrapper, GSD matcher, settings guardian, firestore rules guard, loop detector, deploy safeguard, test tracker, large file gate)
notes: No canonical counterpart. Dense SoNash-specific hook implementation history. KEY TECHNICAL FACTS are portable Claude Code platform facts: exit code 2 = BLOCK for PreToolUse, hooks NOT hot-reloaded mid-session, Windows paths POSIX-normalized before if matching, SKIP_GATES=1 kill switch pattern, PostToolUseFailure is a supported event with if support. These are platform constraints that apply to any Claude Code project. Research findings (if works with ALL tools, template syntax not supported, if is PERFORMANCE not security) are platform facts. recency_signal: 'Research completed Session #244 (2026-03-29)'. ensure-fnm.sh timing: 234ms → 89ms via lean wrapper.
portable_elements: Technical platform facts are HIGH-value for JASON-OS: exit code 2 = BLOCK, no hot-reload, Windows POSIX normalization, SKIP_GATES=1 pattern, PostToolUseFailure event support. 'if is PERFORMANCE not security' is a critical design principle. Template syntax not supported ({{ }}) is a common misconception to prevent.

name: Learning system analysis
purpose: Key findings from learning system brainstorm + deep-research: 89.2% metric is vanity, graduation pipeline has 3 bugs, code vs behavioral enforcement are different systems.
notes: No canonical counterpart. Mix of SoNash-specific metrics and portable findings. METR 2025 study (perceived 20% speedup, actual 19% slowdown) is an external research finding — high-value portable data point. ITS (Interrupted Time Series) as canonical single-group before/after method is portable. 'No AI tool proves learning effectiveness — industry norm' is a portable meta-finding. Cursor killed memories after 5 months (referenced in project_multi_layer_memory.md) adds context. recency_signal: '2026-04-03'.
portable_elements: METR 2025 finding, ITS methodology, 'industry norm is no proof' observation, and 'measure before building' principle are highly portable. Classification distinction (code enforcement vs behavioral learning as structurally different systems) is architecturally portable for any learning system design.

name: Multi-layer memory research
purpose: Deep-research findings on multi-layer memory enhancement: 128 claims verified. Key finding: measure first, enhance existing. AutoDream active. Token cost ~4K always + ~15K on-demand.
notes: No canonical counterpart. Dense research findings with SoNash-specific metrics (44 files, 23-file divergence, T0 actions). KEY PORTABLE FINDINGS: 4K always-injected tokens (CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md index), up to 19,240 if all files loaded (individual files loaded on-demand); 29% abandonment rate is #1 constraint for new mechanisms; A-MAC paper (arxiv:2603.04549) is real but '70% elimination' was fabricated; codebase-memory-mcp (3/5) and Engram (2/5) pre-stable with Windows bugs; Cursor killed memories after 5 months. The T0 priority actions are SoNash-specific paths. recency_signal: '2026-03-31'.
portable_elements: Token cost model (4K always + up to 15K on-demand) is directly applicable to JASON-OS memory design. 29% abandonment rate constraint is universal. Tool stability ratings (codebase-memory-mcp, Engram) portable. A-MAC paper reference portable. Cursor memory death warning portable.
